attacking_style: Pure sprinter who does not attack but relies on positioning in final kilometers. Wins from reduced bunch sprints when attrition eliminates pure fastmen.
team_tactics: Team sets tempo on front to control approaches, but rider frequently becomes isolated in finals without dedicated lead-out train support, forcing early self-positioning.
terrain_preference: Reduced bunch sprints after late splits from technical finishes or roadside furniture. Struggles when pure sprint trains dominate organized finales.
vulnerability: Isolated without lead-out support in organized sprints. Missing from final positioning suggests timing/placement issues when teams execute structured trains. Quick-Step lack of lead-out infrastructure problematic.
key_patterns: Becomes isolated in sprint finals without lead-out, forces early self-positioning (2 observations)
Capitalizes on chaotic/reduced sprints when favorites boxed or eliminated by splits (2 observations)
Team provides front tempo work but not structured lead-out train (2 observations)
Positioning or timing failures evident when missing from organized sprint analysis (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: showed rapid recovery post-crash in 2017, but 2018 Vuelta reveals persistent isolation issues despite stage win
